mission.title: podman-desktop: Podman Desktop setup failed on requirement check: WSL version should be >= 1.2.5.
mission.description: Podman Desktop setup failed on requirement check: WSL version should be >= 1.2.5.. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current podman-desktop deployment
steps.description:
Verify your podman-desktop version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
helm list -n podman-desktop 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working podman-desktop installation.

steps.title: Review podman-desktop configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant podman-desktop configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
kubectl get configmap -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=podman-desktop
```
### Bug description

I tried to install Podman Desktop on my Windows 11 machine. I have enabled Virtualization and WSL2, yet it is failed at WSL version check. I've made sure that WSL2 is installed (wsl version check provided below).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Podman Desktop setup failed on requirement check: WSL…
steps.description:
it is detected as invalid command.
perhaps the command has been deprecated?

what was the expected output of `wsl --version`?

```
> wsl --version
Invalid command line option: --version
Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.

Usage: wsl.exe [Argument] [Options...]
```yaml
❌ WSL Version

Details:
WSL version should be >= 1.2.5.
```

steps.title: Upgrade podman-desktop to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade podman-desktop:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade podman-desktop podman-desktop/podman-desktop --namespace podman-desktop
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n podman-desktop
helm list -n podman-desktop
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
kubectl get events -n podman-desktop --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Podman Desktop setup failed on requirement check: WSL…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
it is detected as invalid command.
perhaps the command has been deprecated?

what was the expected output of `wsl --version`?

```
> wsl --version
Invalid command line option: --version
Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
codeSnippets:
❌ WSL Version

Details:
WSL version should be >= 1.2.5.

> wsl --status
Default Distribution: Ubuntu-22.04

Windows Subsystem for Linux was last updated on 11/23/2023
WSL automatic updates are on.

Kernel version: 5.10.16

> wsl --version

Usage: wsl.exe [Argument] [Options...] [CommandLine]

Arguments for running Linux binaries:

    If no command line is provided, wsl.exe launches the default shell.

    --exec, -e <CommandLine>
        Execute the specified command without using the default Linux shell.

    --
        Pass the remaining command line as is.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with podman-desktop installed or the issue environment reproducible.
